description: Registry of all legal sources, regulations and guidelines used in the WHT Beneficial Owner Agent. Each entry documents provenance, verification status, agent tool dependencies, and RAG readiness for future ingestion.

source_types.database: Reference database or lookup table (derived or official)

statuses.current: Verified current; used in agent logic
statuses.pending_verification: Source exists; individual provisions not yet confirmed against original
statuses.superseded: Replaced by a newer version; kept for historical context
statuses.planned: Identified as needed; not yet integrated into agent

sources.name: Ustawa z dnia 15 lutego 1992 r. o podatku dochodowym od osób prawnych
key_provisions: Art. 4a pkt 29 — Beneficial owner definition (3 cumulative conditions)
Art. 21 ust. 1 — WHT rates on interest and royalties (20%)
Art. 21 ust. 3 — EU I&R Directive implementation (0% exemption)
Art. 22 ust. 1 — WHT rate on dividends (19%)
Art. 22 ust. 4 — EU P-S Directive implementation (0% exemption)
Art. 22c — General Anti-Avoidance Rule (GAAR) for WHT
Art. 26 — Remitter obligations, beneficial owner verification, due diligence
Art. 26 §2c — Pay and Refund mechanism (PLN 2M threshold)
Art. 26aa — Withholding and refund procedure
Art. 26b — Opinion on WHT Exemption (Opinia o stosowaniu preferencji)
Art. 11a — Transfer pricing related-party definition
sources.rag_notes: Full act is ~150 pages; for WHT agent, extract chapters on Art. 4a, 21, 22, 26 only (~20 pages). High-priority for RAG Phase 9.
sources.notes: URL uses 'Lj' suffix (ostatni jednolity) — ISAP updates the file in place. Always the latest consolidation under the 1992 act record. Frank has verified the current DzU reference.

sources.name: Ustawa z dnia 29 sierpnia 1997 r. Ordynacja podatkowa
sources.amendment_ref: Dz.U. 2026 poz. 347 (post-consolidation amendment — must be read together)
key_provisions: Art. 14a-14m — Binding tax interpretations and explanations (ochrona podatkowa)
Art. 14k-14m — Protection for taxpayers following official guidance
Art. 14p — Protection extended to withholding agents (płatnicy)
Art. 30 §5a — Related-party: withholding agent liability not excluded by taxpayer fault
sources.rag_notes: Most relevant for agent: arts. 14a-14p on protection status. Low priority for RAG — only procedural impact on agent.
sources.notes: Both poz. 252 and poz. 347 must be read together.

sources.name: Council Directive 2003/49/EC of 3 June 2003 on a common system of taxation applicable to interest and royalty payments made between associated companies of different Member States
sources.short_name: EU Interest and Royalties Directive (I&R Directive)
key_provisions: Art. 1 — Exemption from withholding tax on cross-border interest and royalty payments
Art. 3 — Associated companies definition (≥25% direct shareholding, ≥2 years)
Art. 5 — Anti-abuse provision (Directive does not preclude domestic GAAR)
sources.rag_notes: Short directive (~6 pages); good RAG candidate. Pair with Art. 21 CIT Act.
sources.notes: The 0% rate applies only when: EU member state recipient, ≥25% shareholding, ≥2 continuous years, income type is interest or royalty (NOT dividends).

sources.name: Council Directive 2011/96/EU of 30 November 2011 on the common system of taxation applicable in the case of parent companies and subsidiaries of different Member States (recast)
key_provisions: Art. 5 — Prohibition of withholding tax on profit distributions from subsidiary to parent
Art. 3 — Parent company definition (≥10% shareholding, 24 months minimum)
Art. 1(2) — General anti-abuse clause (GAAR)
sources.rag_notes: Short directive; good RAG candidate. Pair with Art. 22 ust. 4 CIT Act.
sources.notes: Applies to dividends only (not interest/royalties). Threshold: ≥10% direct shareholding, ≥2 continuous years.

sources.name: Objaśnienia podatkowe z 3 lipca 2025 r. dotyczące stosowania tzw. klauzuli rzeczywistego właściciela dla celów podatku u źródła
key_provisions: §2.2.1 — Conduit indicators (red flags) for conditions (i)+(ii)
§2.3 — Universal substance factors for condition (iii)
§2.3.1 — Holding companies: lower substance threshold + shared substance rules
§3 — Payment types requiring BO check (dividends, interest, royalties only)
§4 — Due diligence standards (related vs. unrelated parties)
§6.2 — Look-Through Approach (LTA) rules
§6.3 — Extended subject scope
§6.4 — PS Directive presumption
§6.5 — OZZ (collective rights management organisations)
sources.protection_status: Full legal protection under Art. 14k-14m Tax Ordinance for taxpayers and withholding agents (Art. 14p) who follow these explanations.
sources.rag_notes: Primary target for RAG Phase 9. ~60 pages. Ingesting this document would replace the hardcoded substance simulation with real authoritative logic.
sources.notes: The most authoritative domestic source on the BO test. Has legal protection status — agents referencing it are in the safest position.

sources.name: OECD Model Tax Convention on Income and on Capital (2017, condensed version)
key_provisions: Art. 7 — Business profits
Art. 10 — Dividends (BO requirement, Commentary evolution 1977-2014)
Art. 11 — Interest (BO requirement)
Art. 12 — Royalties (BO requirement; note: not all Polish treaties follow OECD Art. 12)
Art. 26 — Exchange of information
sources.rag_notes: Commentary sections on Arts. 10-12 are the most relevant (~50 pages). Medium priority for RAG.
sources.notes: Key for BO interpretation history. The 2014 Commentary update introduced the 'facts and circumstances' test for pass-through obligations and the dependent/independent payment distinction — both used in MF Objaśnienia 2025.

sources.name: Multilateral Convention to Implement Tax Treaty Related Measures to Prevent Base Erosion and Profit Shifting
key_provisions: Art. 7 — Principal Purpose Test (PPT) — anti-abuse rule for treaty benefits
Art. 6 — Preamble language (treaty purpose: no double non-taxation)
Art. 8 — Dividend holding period (Poland reserved for treaties already containing holding period)
Art. 10 — PE third-country abuse (Poland reserved — does NOT apply)
Art. 12 — Commissionnaire PE (Poland reserved — does NOT apply)
sources.rag_notes: Low priority for RAG — Poland's positions are captured in the project's data files and treaties.json.
sources.notes: The MLI modifies bilateral treaties; it does not replace them. Effect depends on both parties' notified CTAs and specific reservations.

sources.name: Poland's MLI Deposited Positions (Notification under Art. 35)
key_facts: 78 Covered Tax Agreements (CTAs) notified by Poland
Art. 7 PPT accepted as interim measure for all 78 CTAs
Germany: explicitly excluded from Poland's CTAs — MLI PPT does NOT apply
Art. 8 reserved for treaties already containing holding period (Belgium, Cyprus, Denmark, Luxembourg, Malta, Norway, Portugal, Singapore, Slovakia, Switzerland, UK)
sources.rag_notes: Data extracted into data/poland_dtt_list.csv — the project's CSV is the primary lookup. PDF kept for audit trail.
sources.notes: Frank personally verified and cross-checked. Key finding: Germany is the only major EU country excluded from Poland's CTAs.

sources.name: MLI Signatories and Parties — OECD Official Status Table
key_facts: 83+ Parties (ratified and in force) as of Jan 2026
Italy: signed 2017 but NOT ratified as of Jan 2026 → MLI PPT does NOT apply for Poland-Italy
sources.rag_notes: Data extracted into poland_dtt_list.csv. Not needed for RAG.
sources.notes: Frank personally verified. Cross-checked with Poland's positions to derive mli_ppt_applies values.

sources.name: OECD BEPS MLI Matching Database
key_facts: Per-treaty tool: shows whether both parties included a specific treaty as a CTA
Needed to resolve VERIFY cases in treaties.json (Netherlands, Sweden, Switzerland)
sources.rag_notes: Interactive web tool — not suitable for direct RAG. Use to resolve VERIFY cases and update treaties.json.
sources.notes: VERIFY cases: Netherlands, Sweden, Switzerland. Absence of synthesized text after 6+ years strongly implies those countries excluded the Poland treaty from their own CTAs. Must be confirmed here before updating mli_ppt_applies to 'NO' for those entries.

sources.name: Wykaz umów o unikaniu podwójnego opodatkowania — Poland MoF treaty list
key_facts: 91 bilateral tax treaties in force (as of Jan 2026)
Used as the canonical list; 36 countries extracted into treaties.json
sources.rag_notes: Web page — not suitable for RAG. Periodic check needed to catch newly ratified treaties.
sources.notes: Frank confirmed list. 91 entries in poland_dtt_list.csv. treaties.json covers 36 most commercially relevant.

sources.name: OECD Transfer Pricing Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises and Tax Administrations 2022 — Revised Chapter VI: Intangibles
key_provisions: Chapter VI — Intangibles: legal vs. economic ownership of IP
Section D.1 — DEMPE framework: Development, Enhancement, Maintenance, Protection, Exploitation
Section D.2 — DEMPE control: entity bearing risk/cost of DEMPE entitled to return
Chapter IX — Business restructurings (relevance: IP transfers pre-royalty payment)
Paras 6.32-6.54 — Economic ownership test for intangibles
sources.rag_notes: High priority for RAG Phase 9. Ingesting Chapter VI and relevant DEMPE sections (~80 pages) would substantially improve the analyse_dempe tool.
sources.notes: The DEMPE framework from BEPS Actions 8-10 is the legal basis for the analyse_dempe tool. Economic ownership of IP (not legal title) determines entitlement to royalty as BO.

sources.name: Test substancji ekonomicznej w przypadku spółek pośredniczących – klauzula rzeczywistego właściciela a recharakteryzacja transakcji
key_insights: BO clause is binary (qualifying) — entity IS or IS NOT the BO; NOT a gradient assessment
BO test ≠ TP recharacterisation — different thresholds, different instruments
Obligation to pass on = factual OR contractual (CJEU Danish Cases 2019)
CJEU Danish Cases (C-116/16, C-117/16 et al.) — foundation for Polish substance test
NSA Nov 2023 (II FSK 27/23): DutchCo denied BO — capital/income disproportion
IKEA Netherlands 2022 (CAA Versailles): BO confirmed with pass-through if genuine business
Austria VwGH 2020: formal IP ownership insufficient without functional control
sources.rag_notes: Excellent RAG target for case law queries. 27 pages of dense legal analysis. Pair with MF Objaśnienia 2025.
sources.notes: Primary doctrinal source for the substance test logic in check_entity_substance. Frank has the PDF; verified personally.

sources.name: Project treaty database — derived static lookup table
sources.coverage: 36 countries: EU-27 + UK, Switzerland, Norway, USA, Canada, Japan, Singapore, UAE, Australia, India
key_facts: 36 entries with treaty metadata, MLI flags, and rates
All rates marked verified: false — from professional commentary, not official PDFs
mli_ppt_applies: YES / NO / VERIFY per country
VERIFY cases: Netherlands, Sweden, Switzerland — need OECD Matching DB check
verification_priority: Germany — confirmed NO PPT, rates need PDF check
Netherlands, Sweden, Switzerland — VERIFY MLI status
sources.rag_notes: Already machine-readable JSON — can be loaded as a context document for treaty queries. Suitable as a lookup context for a RAG-augmented treaty tool.
sources.notes: This is the agent's primary live data source for three tools. Highest priority for data quality improvement.

sources.name: Poland DTT list with MLI cross-check — full 91-country CSV
sources.authority: Project-maintained (derived from MoF list + OECD MLI positions)
sources.rag_notes: CSV format — would need preprocessing for RAG. The 36-country subset in treaties.json is more suitable.
sources.notes: Frank personally compiled from MoF treaty list + OECD MLI positions. Full cross-check completed March 2026.

planned_sources.name: Umowa między Rządem PRL a Rządem Republiki Francuskiej w sprawie zapobiegania podwójnemu opodatkowaniu w zakresie podatków od dochodu i majątku
planned_sources.notes: Key case: Orange Polska. Art. 12 scope may not cover all modern royalties — Art. 7 Business Profits fallback possible. Needs PDF verification for rates and Art. 12 definition.

planned_sources.name: Umowa między Rządem RP a Rządem Wielkiego Księstwa Luksemburga w sprawie unikania podwójnego opodatkowania (2012 Protocol)
planned_sources.notes: 2012 Protocol changed dividend rates. Verify current rates against DzU reference in treaties.json.

planned_sources.name: Umowa między RP a Republiką Federalną Niemiec w sprawie unikania podwójnego opodatkowania (2003)
planned_sources.notes: Germany excluded from Poland's MLI CTAs — PPT confirmed NO. Rates need PDF verification.

planned_sources.name: Konwencja między RP a Królestwem Niderlandów w sprawie unikania podwójnego opodatkowania
planned_sources.notes: VERIFY MLI case — check OECD Matching Database. Rates need PDF verification.

rag_roadmap.description: Phased plan for moving from static data to RAG-based treaty and legal knowledge

phases.benefit: Replace hardcoded substance simulation with authoritative BO test logic. Most impactful single improvement.
phases.approach: Chunk by section; embed with topic labels; retrieve by condition and entity type

phases.benefit: Replace hardcoded DEMPE simulation with real OECD Ch. VI functional analysis.
phases.approach: Chunk Chapter VI by DEMPE function; embed with IP type and function labels

phases.benefit: Improve directive exemption analysis from hardcoded rules to cited-text reasoning.
phases.approach: Short documents; load in full as context rather than chunk-and-retrieve

phases.benefit: Rate verification and Art. 12 scope checks against official treaty PDFs.
phases.approach: Per-treaty chunks; retrieve by article number and income type
